```{r}
  geom_text(aes(label = stars, color = Estimate), size = 8, fontface = "bold", vjust = 0.7) +
  
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) stringr::str_wrap(x, width = 35)) +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#D55E00", mid = "white", high = "#56B4E9",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-max_abs, max_abs),
                       name = "Regression\nEstimate") +
  
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "white", mid = "gray20", high = "white",
                        midpoint = 0, limits = c(-max_abs, max_abs),
                        guide = "none") + 
  
  labs(title    = "Microbial Pathway-Metabolite Associations",
       subtitle = "Top 20 Pathways by FDR | * p<0.05  ** p<0.01  *** p<0.001",
       x = "Metabolite", y = "Pathway") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 29),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 22, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 20, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8), 
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.title  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.text   = element_text(size = 22),
        panel.grid    = element_blank(),
        plot.margin   = margin(t = 10, r = 30, b = 20, l = 10))
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Heatmap_Top20.png"), heatmap_plot, width = 16, height = 18, dpi = 300)
 
# Top 20 pathway-metabolite pairs bar plot 
top20 <- head(high_conf_path_met[order(high_conf_path_met$p_adjusted), ], 20)
top20$pathway_readable <- clean_pathway_name(top20$pathway)
top20$pair_label <- paste0(
  str_trunc(top20$pathway_readable, 40),
  "  ->  ",
  top20$metabolite,
  "  ", top20$stars
)
 
pair_plot <- ggplot(top20, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(pair_label, Estimate),
                                fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"),
                    labels = c("Decreased with Pathway Activity",
                               "Increased with Pathway Activity"),
                    name   = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title    = "Top 20 Pathway-Metabolite Associations",
       subtitle = "High-Confidence Pairs (FDR & Spearman p < 0.05)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 18, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        plot.margin     = margin(t = 10, r = 30, b = 10, l = 10))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Top20.png"), pair_plot, width = 18, height = 11, dpi = 300)
 
```

## Step 6: Mediation Analysis

So step 5 shows that certain metabolites/pathways are correlated with S. aureus level. This mediation analysis will see if those metabolites/pathways are actually used by SA to drive skin lesionality.

-   Independent variable = SA level

-   Mediator = metabolites/pathways

-   Outcome = lesional vs non lesional

Will run a loop for each metabolite and pathway to see if any are significant mediators. Using bootstrapped confidence intervals (n=5000 permutations), ending up with an estimate of the indirect effect to build the CI.

Scaled (Z-scores) the pathways to handle the tiny abundance numbers. Used logistic regression because lesional is binary.

```{r}
# Rebuild meta_cols to include sa_abundance
meta_cols <- master_table[, c("NMR_sample_id", "sa_abundance", "SA_status", "lesional", "pH_mean")]

# Rebuild plot_master and plot_pathways with the corrected meta_cols
nmr_df <- as.data.frame(nmr_matched, check.names = FALSE)
nmr_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(nmr_df)
plot_master <- merge(nmr_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")

path_df <- as.data.frame(pathway_matched, check.names = FALSE)
path_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(path_df)
plot_pathways <- merge(path_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")

"sa_abundance" %in% colnames(plot_master)  # should be TRUE


cat("plot_master rows:", nrow(plot_master), "\n")
cat("plot_pathways rows:", nrow(plot_pathways), "\n")
# Fix the metadata in plotting objects
plot_master$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_master$pH_mean))
plot_pathways$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_pathways$pH_mean))

# Define the Master Mediation Function 
# This handles both Metabolites (Standard) and Pathways (Scaled)
# Create a list to store results
mediation_summary_list <- list()

# Get the list of significant metabolites 
target_metabolites <- metabolite_hits$feature 


run_sa_mediation_stable <- function(feature, data, is_pathway = FALSE,
                                     treat_col = "sa_abundance",
                                     outcome_col = "lesional",
                                     covariate_col = "pH_mean",
                                     sims = 5000,
                                     seed = 123) {

  
  df <- data.frame(
    mediator  = as.numeric(as.character(data[[feature]])),
    treat     = as.numeric(as.character(data[[treat_col]])),
    outcome   = data[[outcome_col]],
    covariate = as.numeric(as.character(data[[covariate_col]]))
  )


  df$outcome <- ifelse(toupper(as.character(df$outcome)) %in% c("LES", "LESIONAL"), 1, 0)

  
  if (is_pathway) {
    df$mediator <- as.numeric(scale(df$mediator))
  }

 
  df <- df[complete.cases(df), ]

  n_used <- nrow(df)


  if (n_used < 10 || length(unique(df$outcome)) < 2) {
    stop(paste0("Insufficient data for mediation (n = ", n_used,
                 ", outcome levels = ", length(unique(df$outcome)), ")"))
  }

 
  model_m <- lm(mediator ~ treat + covariate, data = df)

  model_y <- glm(outcome ~ mediator + treat + covariate,
                  data = df, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

  set.seed(seed)
  med_out <- mediation::mediate(
    model.m = model_m,
    model.y = model_y,
    treat        = "treat",
    mediator     = "mediator",
    boot         = TRUE,     
    sims         = sims,      
    boot.ci.type = "perc"
  )

  med_out$nobs <- n_used

  return(med_out)
}

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_metabolites), "metabolites..."))

for(met in target_metabolites) {
  tryCatch({
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(met, plot_master, is_pathway = FALSE)
    
    # Extract the key numbers
    sum_med <- summary(med_out)
    
    mediation_summary_list[[met]] <- data.frame(
      Metabolite = met,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", met))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", met, ":", e$message))
  })
}
# Check if the list is empty
if (length(mediation_summary_list) == 0) {
  stop("The list is empty! No mediation results were captured.")
}

# Check for potential NULLs (e.g. if a loop didn't add a data frame)
mediation_summary_list <- mediation_summary_list[!sapply(mediation_summary_list, is.null)]

if (length(mediation_summary_list) == 0) {
  stop("All entries in the list were NULL.")
}

# Combine 
mediation_results_table <- do.call(rbind, mediation_summary_list)

# Final check: verify if the expected column exists before sorting
if (!"ACME_P_Value" %in% colnames(mediation_results_table)) {
  stop("The column 'ACME_P_Value' is missing. Please inspect the structure of one list entry using: str(mediation_summary_list[[1]])")
}

#  Sort 
mediation_results_table <- mediation_results_table[order(mediation_results_table$ACME_P_Value), ]
#  Combine into one table
mediation_results_table <- do.call(rbind, mediation_summary_list)

# View and Sort by P-value
mediation_results_table <- mediation_results_table[order(mediation_results_table$ACME_P_Value), ]
print(mediation_results_table)

# Save the table
write.csv(mediation_results_table, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Metabolites.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Create a list to store results
mediation_pathway_list <- list()

top_path_df <- head(pathway_hits[order(pathway_hits$p_value), ], 15)

# Get the top 5 pathways (already sorted by p-value )
target_pathways <- head(top_path_df$feature, 15)

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_pathways), "pathways..."))

for(path in target_pathways) {
  tryCatch({
    # Run mediation function with is_pathway = TRUE
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(path, plot_pathways, is_pathway = TRUE)
    
    # Extract key statistics
    mediation_pathway_list[[path]] <- data.frame(
      Pathway = path,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef,
      Samples_Used = med_out$nobs
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", path))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", path, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# Combine into one table
mediation_pathway_results <- do.call(rbind, mediation_pathway_list)

# Sort by Mediation P-value (ACME)
mediation_pathway_results <- mediation_pathway_results[order(mediation_pathway_results$ACME_P_Value), ]

# Print 
print(mediation_pathway_results)

#Save 
write.csv(mediation_pathway_results, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Pathways.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```

# Tier 2: scRNA-seq Mechanistic Subset

### Load Single Cell RNA-seq Data

Loading the merged Seurat object

```{r}
# Path
file_path <- "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/r_adata_microbiome.rds"

# Load data
if (file.exists(file_path)) {
  load(gzfile(file_path))
  message("Success: Object 'adata' loaded into environment.")
} else {
  stop("File not found. Please check the OneDrive sync status.")
}


# Check dimensions and layers
print(adata) 
# Check the first few features
head(rownames(adata))
```

### QC

```{r}
all_counts <- JoinLayers(adata[["RNA"]])
adata_new <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = all_counts, project = "Epidermis_QC")

# attach metadata 
meta_to_attach <- adata@meta.data
rownames(meta_to_attach) <- colnames(adata_new)
adata_new@meta.data <- meta_to_attach

# Calculate Mitochondrial percentage
adata_new[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(adata_new, pattern = "^MT-")

# Apply QC filtering
# Keep cells with > 200 genes AND < 5% mitochondrial DNA
adata_qc <- subset(adata_new, subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & percent.mt < 5)

# FINAL VERIFICATION 
cat("\n QC SUMMARY \n")
print(paste("Total cells after QC:", ncol(adata_qc)))

cat("\n LESIONAL VS NON-LESIONAL SPLIT\n")
print(table(adata_qc$les))

cat("\n CELL COUNTS PER CLUSTER \n")
print(table(adata_qc$clusters))

cat("\n SAMPLE CONTRIBUTION BY STATUS \n")
print(table(adata_qc$sample, adata_qc$les))

```

## Step 6: DEG for SA High vs Low

Differential expression analysis comparing High vs Low SA groups.

| Patient ID | Lesional sample | Non-lesional sample | SA abundance (both sites) | SA status |
|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| GYECZ1191 | A17 (les) | A18 (non_les) | 49.3 / 67.5 | High |
| GYECZ1193 | A21 (les) | A22 (non_les) | 89.3 / 57.3 | High |
| GYECZ1197 | A38 (les) | A37 (non_les) | 0.0 / 2.5 | Low |

### 6.1 Build SA Status & Bridge to scRNA Samples

```{r}
master_table <- master_table %>%
  mutate(SA_status = case_when(
    sa_abundance > 35 ~ "High",
    sa_abundance < 10 ~ "Low",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # 10-35% range excluded
  ))
 
Clusters <- read.csv("~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/r_clusters.csv")

load("~/Desktop/iawards/metadata/seq_metadata_filt.RData")
 
# Only these 6 scRNA samples currently have linked microbiome data.
# Note: these 6 samples come from 3 patients (see sample_to_patient below).
target_ids <- c("A17", "A18", "A21", "A22", "A37", "A38")
 
bridge_clean <- as.data.frame(seq_metadata_filt) %>%
  mutate(scRNA_ID = regmatches(as.character(seq_id), regexpr("A[0-9]+", as.character(seq_id)))) %>%
  filter(scRNA_ID %in% target_ids) %>%
  dplyr::select(scRNA_ID, eg_id) %>%
  distinct()
 
sa_status_map <- master_table %>%
  mutate(eg_id = as.character(eg_id)) %>%
  left_join(bridge_clean, by = "eg_id", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  dplyr::select(sample = scRNA_ID, SA_status) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sample), !is.na(SA_status)) %>%
  distinct()
 
adata_qc$SA_status <- sa_status_map$SA_status[match(adata_qc$sample, sa_status_map$sample)]
table(adata_qc$SA_status, useNA = "always")
 
# True patient mapping: A17/A18, A21/A22, A37/A38 are paired lesional /
# non-lesional biopsies from the SAME three individuals
# SA status is constant within each patient

sample_to_patient <- data.frame(
  sample       = c("A17","A18","A21","A22","A37","A38"),
  true_patient = c("GYECZ1191","GYECZ1191","GYECZ1193","GYECZ1193","GYECZ1197","GYECZ1197")
)
 
adata_qc$true_patient <- sample_to_patient$true_patient[match(adata_qc$sample, sample_to_patient$sample)]
 
# Subset to High/Low only
adata_sa <- subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status %in% c("High", "Low"))
adata_sa$SA_status <- factor(adata_sa$SA_status, levels = c("Low", "High"))
 
# Check: does adata_sa actually have true_patient set correctly?
table(adata_sa$sample, adata_sa$true_patient, useNA = "always")
 
```

### 6.2 Pseudobulk & Differential Expression Analysis

```{r}
# PATIENT-LEVEL PSEUDOBULK
# Aggregating by patient (not sample) avoids treating the same person's
# two biopsies as independent replicates for the SA comparison.
 
pseudo_counts_sa <- AggregateExpression(adata_sa,
                                        group.by = c("clusters", "true_patient", "SA_status"),
                                        assays = "RNA",
                                        return.seurat = FALSE)
 
mat_sa <- pseudo_counts_sa$RNA
dim(mat_sa)  # expect up to 11 clusters x 3 patients = 33, fewer if a cluster is missing one patient's cells 
 
# DESeq2 LOOP
 
all_clusters_sa <- unique(adata_sa$clusters)
 
output_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_sa)) dir.create(output_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)
 
for (cluster in all_clusters_sa) {
 
  message("Processing cluster: ", cluster)
 
  cluster_cols <- grep(paste0("^", cluster, "_"), colnames(mat_sa), value = TRUE)
 
  # Requires all 3 patients represented for this cluster.
  if (length(cluster_cols) < 3) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster, ": not enough patients represented.")
    next
  }
 
  cluster_mat <- mat_sa[, cluster_cols]
 
  cluster_meta <- data.frame(ColName = cluster_cols) %>%
    mutate(
      Patient  = str_extract(ColName, "GYECZ[0-9]+"),
      SA_group = ifelse(grepl("_High$", ColName), "High", "Low"),
      Cluster  = str_remove(ColName, paste0("_", Patient, "_.*$"))
    )
  cluster_meta$SA_group <- factor(cluster_meta$SA_group, levels = c("Low", "High"))
  rownames(cluster_meta) <- cluster_cols
 
  if (length(unique(cluster_meta$SA_group)) < 2) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster, ": only one SA group present.")
    next
  }
 
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cluster_mat,
                                colData   = cluster_meta,
                                design    = ~ SA_group)
 
  dds <- DESeq(dds, quiet = TRUE)
 
  coef_name <- grep("SA_group", resultsNames(dds), value = TRUE)
 
  # Shrinkage worthwhile given how small n is. Warnings
  # from apeglm's optimizer are expected with n=1 in the Low group and don't
  # indicate broken code 
  res_shrunk <- suppressWarnings(lfcShrink(dds, coef = coef_name, type = "apeglm"))
 
  clean_name <- gsub(" ", "_", cluster)
  write.csv(as.data.frame(res_shrunk),
            paste0(output_dir_sa, "DEG_SA_Results_", clean_name, ".csv"))
 
  message("Saved: ", clean_name)
}
```

### 6.4 Validation of DEg Count and Cluster Size

```{r}
# DEG COUNT vs CLUSTER SIZE DIAGNOSTIC
# Checks how much of the
# variation in "number of DEGs per cluster" is just statistical power
# tracking cell count, rather than biological relevance.
 
cluster_sizes <- table(adata_sa$clusters)
names(cluster_sizes) <- gsub(" ", "_", names(cluster_sizes))
 
deg_counts <- sapply(deg_files_sa, function(f) {
  df <- read.csv(f, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1)
  nrow(df)
})
names(deg_counts) <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(deg_files_sa))
 
comparison <- data.frame(
  cluster = names(deg_counts),
  n_DEGs  = as.numeric(deg_counts),
  n_cells = as.numeric(cluster_sizes[names(deg_counts)])
)
 
cor(comparison$n_DEGs, comparison$n_cells, use = "complete.obs")
print(comparison)
 
```

```{r}
# Visualisations 

deg_files_sa <- list.files(output_dir_sa,
                            pattern = "DEG_SA_Results_.*\\.csv",
                            full.names = TRUE)


# VOLCANO PLOT LOOP - SA HIGH vs LOW
volcano_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/Volcano_Plots/"
if (!dir.exists(volcano_dir_sa)) dir.create(volcano_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)

for (file in deg_files_sa) {

  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("Plotting: ", cluster_name)

  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange)) %>%
    mutate(
      gene = rownames(.),
      sig  = case_when(
        padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange >  1 ~ "Upregulated in SA High",
        padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Upregulated in SA Low",
        TRUE                               ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      label = ifelse(padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1, gene, NA)
    )

  if (nrow(res_df) < 10) next

  p <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = sig), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text_repel(
      aes(label = label),
      size                = 5,
      max.overlaps        = Inf,
      box.padding         = 0.5,
      point.padding       = 0.3,
      segment.size        = 0.3,
      segment.alpha       = 0.5,
      min.segment.length  = 0.2,
      force               = 2,
      seed                = 42
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
      "Upregulated in SA High"                          = "#e41a1c",
      "Upregulated in SA Low"                            = "royalblue",
      "Not significant"                                  = "grey60"
    )) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-5, 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste("SA High (>35) vs SA Low (<10):", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
      x        = "log2 Fold Change",
      y        = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
      colour   = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid       = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "top",
      legend.direction = "vertical",
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 14)
    )

  ggsave(filename = paste0(volcano_dir_sa, "Volcano_SA_", cluster_name, ".png"),
         plot   = p,
         width  = 10,
         height = 9,
         dpi    = 300)
}

# DOT PLOTS - SA HIGH vs LOW


adata_sa <- NormalizeData(adata_sa, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)
adata_sa$cluster_SA <- paste0(adata_sa$clusters, "_", adata_sa$SA_status)

plot_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/DotPlots/"
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir_sa)) dir.create(plot_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)

# Collect cluster-specific significant DEGs (recurring genes excluded), scale once
all_sig_genes <- c()
for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1)
  all_sig_genes <- c(all_sig_genes, rownames(res_df))
}
all_sig_genes <- setdiff(unique(all_sig_genes), recurring_genes)
all_sig_genes <- intersect(all_sig_genes, rownames(adata_sa))
adata_sa <- ScaleData(adata_sa, features = all_sig_genes)

for (file in deg_files_sa) {

  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("Plotting dotplot for: ", cluster_name)

  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
    filter(!gene %in% recurring_genes) %>%
    arrange(padj)

  if (nrow(res_df) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": no cluster-specific DEGs (after removing recurring genes).")
    next
  }

  sig_genes   <- head(res_df$gene, 20)
  valid_genes <- intersect(sig_genes, rownames(adata_sa))

  if (length(valid_genes) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": no DEGs found in Seurat object.")
    next
  }

  # this plots cluster_SA across the WHOLE object, so other cell types'
  # High/Low groups will also appear on the y-axis alongside this cluster's.
  p <- DotPlot(adata_sa,
               features = valid_genes,
               group.by = "cluster_SA",
               cols     = c("blue", "red"),
               scale    = TRUE,
               col.min  = -1.5,
               col.max  =  1.5) +
    RotatedAxis() +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey95"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(title    = paste("Cluster-specific DEGs:", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
         subtitle = paste0("S. aureus High (>35) vs Low (<10) | n=", length(valid_genes),
                            " genes (genes recurring in >=", recurrence_threshold,
                            " clusters excluded)"),
         colour   = "Relative Exp.",
         size     = "% Expressed",
         x        = "Significant DEGs",
         y        = "Cell Type & SA Status")

  ggsave(filename = paste0(plot_dir_sa, "DotPlot_SA_DEGs_", cluster_name, ".png"),
         plot = p, width = 14, height = 11, dpi = 300)

  message("Saved dotplot for: ", cluster_name)
}
```

### 6.5. GSEA

```{r}

gsea_base_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/GSEA_Results/SA_HighvsLow/"
dir.create(gsea_base_dir_sa, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

h_df <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", collection = "H")
pathways_h <- split(x = h_df$gene_symbol, f = h_df$gs_name)

deg_files_sa <- list.files(output_dir_sa,
                            pattern = "DEG_SA_Results_.*\\.csv",
                            full.names = TRUE)

for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  
  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("--- Running GSEA for: ", cluster_name, " ---")
  
  # Each cluster gets its own subfolder
  cluster_dir <- paste0(gsea_base_dir_sa, cluster_name, "/")
  dir.create(cluster_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange), !is.na(padj))
  
  if (nrow(res_df) < 15) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": Not enough genes.")
    next
  }
  
  gene_ranks <- res_df$log2FoldChange
  names(gene_ranks) <- rownames(res_df)
  set.seed(123)
  gene_ranks <- gene_ranks + rnorm(length(gene_ranks), mean = 0, sd = 1e-10)
  gene_ranks <- sort(gene_ranks, decreasing = TRUE)
  
  fgsea_res <- fgsea(pathways = pathways_h,
                     stats    = gene_ranks,
                     minSize  = 15,
                     maxSize  = 500)
  
  sig_pathways <- fgsea_res[fgsea_res$padj < 0.05, ]
  sig_pathways <- sig_pathways[order(sig_pathways$NES, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  if (nrow(sig_pathways) == 0) {
    message("No significant pathways for ", cluster_name)
    next
  }
  
  # Save 
  sig_pathways_save <- sig_pathways
  sig_pathways_save$leadingEdge <- sapply(sig_pathways_save$leadingEdge,
                                           function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
  write.csv(as.data.frame(sig_pathways_save),
            paste0(cluster_dir, "GSEA_SA_Significant_Pathways_", cluster_name, ".csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Top pathway enriched in SA High 
  top_high <- sig_pathways[sig_pathways$NES > 0, ]
  if (nrow(top_high) > 0) {
    top_high <- top_high[order(top_high$NES, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    p_high <- plotEnrichment(pathways_h[[top_high$pathway[1]]], gene_ranks) +
      labs(title    = paste0(gsub("_", " ", cluster_name), " | ", top_high$pathway[1]),
           subtitle = paste0("NES = ", round(top_high$NES[1], 2),
                             ", padj = ", round(top_high$padj[1], 3),
                             " | Enriched in SA High")) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
            axis.title    = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text     = element_text(size = 11))
    ggsave(paste0(cluster_dir, "Top_Pathway_SAHigh.pdf"), p_high, width = 8, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Top pathway enriched in SA Low
  top_low <- sig_pathways[sig_pathways$NES < 0, ]
  if (nrow(top_low) > 0) {
    top_low <- top_low[order(top_low$NES, decreasing = FALSE), ]
    p_low <- plotEnrichment(pathways_h[[top_low$pathway[1]]], gene_ranks) +
      labs(title    = paste0(gsub("_", " ", cluster_name), " | ", top_low$pathway[1]),
           subtitle = paste0("NES = ", round(top_low$NES[1], 2),
                             ", padj = ", round(top_low$padj[1], 3),
                             " | Enriched in SA Low")) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
            axis.title    = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text     = element_text(size = 11))
    ggsave(paste0(cluster_dir, "Top_Pathway_SALow.pdf"), p_low, width = 8, height = 6)
  }
  
  #  Summary barplot of all significant pathways
  summary_df <- sig_pathways %>%
    mutate(
      direction     = ifelse(NES > 0, "Enriched in SA High", "Enriched in SA Low"),
      pathway_clean = gsub("HALLMARK_", "", pathway),
      pathway_clean = gsub("_", " ", pathway_clean)
    ) %>%
    arrange(NES)
  
  summary_df$pathway_clean <- factor(summary_df$pathway_clean,
                                      levels = summary_df$pathway_clean)
  
  p_summary <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = NES, y = pathway_clean, fill = direction)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "black") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Enriched in SA High" = "#e41a1c",
                                  "Enriched in SA Low"  = "royalblue")) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title     = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(title    = paste("Significant Hallmark Pathways:", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
         subtitle = "SA High (>35) vs SA Low (<10) | padj < 0.05",
         x        = "Normalised Enrichment Score (NES)",
         y        = NULL)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(cluster_dir, "All_Pathways_Summary.pdf"),
         plot     = p_summary,
         width    = 10,
         height   = 0.4 * nrow(summary_df) + 3,
         dpi      = 300)
  
  message("Success: ", nrow(sig_pathways), " pathways saved for ", cluster_name)
}



col_meta <- data.frame(ColName = colnames(mat_sa)) %>%
  separate(ColName, 
           into = c("Cluster", "Patient", "SA_group"), 
           sep  = "_", 
           extra = "merge") %>%
  filter(SA_group %in% c("High", "Low"))

sample_counts <- col_meta %>%
  group_by(Cluster, SA_group) %>%
  summarise(n_samples = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = SA_group, values_from = n_samples, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(Total = High + Low)

kable(sample_counts, caption = "Pseudobulk samples per cluster by SA status")

write.csv(sample_counts,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/Pseudobulk_Sample_Counts.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Pathway recurrence diagnostic
gsea_files <- list.files(gsea_base_dir_sa,
                          pattern = "GSEA_SA_Significant_Pathways_.*\\.csv",
                          recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

pathway_recurrence <- lapply(gsea_files, function(f) {
  df <- read.csv(f)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  data.frame(pathway = df$pathway,
             cluster = gsub(".*GSEA_SA_Significant_Pathways_|\\.csv", "", basename(f)))
}) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()

pathway_recurrence_summary <- pathway_recurrence %>% dplyr::count(pathway, name = "n_clusters", sort = TRUE)
print(pathway_recurrence_summary)

```

## Step 7: Genome-Scale Metabolic Model Input Preparation

Prepare the GEM input grouped by SA status

```{r}

# --- All samples ---
mean_expr_per_celltype <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(cell_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_per_celltype) <- unique(cell_types)
cat("All samples range:", round(min(mean_expr_per_celltype), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_per_celltype), 3), "\n")

# --- High SA ---
high_idx <- !is.na(sa_status) & sa_status == "High"
cat("High SA cells:", sum(high_idx), "\n")
high_types <- cell_types[high_idx]
mean_expr_high_sa <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(high_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[high_idx & cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_high_sa) <- unique(high_types)
cat("High SA range:", round(min(mean_expr_high_sa), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_high_sa), 3), "\n")

# --- Low SA ---
low_idx <- !is.na(sa_status) & sa_status == "Low"
cat("Low SA cells:", sum(low_idx), "\n")
low_types <- cell_types[low_idx]
mean_expr_low_sa <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(low_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[low_idx & cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_low_sa) <- unique(low_types)
cat("Low SA range:", round(min(mean_expr_low_sa), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_low_sa), 3), "\n\n")

# --- Save ---
out_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/GEM/pyglpk/Data"

write.csv(mean_expr_per_celltype, file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_per_celltype.csv"))
write.csv(mean_expr_high_sa,      file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_high_sa.csv"))
write.csv(mean_expr_low_sa,       file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_low_sa.csv"))

cat("Saved. File sizes:\n")
for (f in c("mean_expr_per_celltype.csv", "mean_expr_high_sa.csv", "mean_expr_low_sa.csv")) {
  fp <- file.path(out_dir, f)
  cat(sprintf("  %s  %.1f MB\n", f, file.size(fp) / 1e6))
}


```
